Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Megadev: A Development Kit for the Sega Mega Drive and Mega CD Hardware 84 upvotes, 5 comments
  2. Baochip-1x: What it is, why I'm doing it now and how it came about 191 upvotes, 26 comments
  3. NMAP in the Movies 48 upvotes, 5 comments
  4. Can I run AI locally? 1340 upvotes, 323 comments
  5. What happens when US economic data becomes unreliable 19 upvotes, 1 comments
  6. Show HN: Channel Surfer – Watch YouTube like it’s cable TV 569 upvotes, 167 comments
  7. Show HN: GitAgent – An open standard that turns any Git repo into an AI agent 16 upvotes, 1 comments
  8. Secure Secrets Management for Cursor Cloud Agents 32 upvotes, 5 comments
  9. Mouser: An open source alternative to Logi-Plus mouse software 401 upvotes, 129 comments
  10. I found 39 Algolia admin keys exposed across open source documentation sites 147 upvotes, 43 comments
  11. Python: The Optimization Ladder 150 upvotes, 46 comments
  12. UBI Is Your Productivity Dividend – The Only Way to All Share What We All Built 9 upvotes, 0 comments
  13. Hammerspoon 329 upvotes, 120 comments
  14. 1M context is now generally available for Opus 4.6 and Sonnet 4.6 1020 upvotes, 423 comments
  15. Cookie jars capture American kitsch (2023) 12 upvotes, 1 comments
  16. Philosoph Jürgen Habermas Gestorben 87 upvotes, 30 comments
  17. Show HN: Ink – Deploy full-stack apps from AI agents via MCP or Skills 15 upvotes, 2 comments
  18. The Isolation Trap: Erlang 115 upvotes, 47 comments
  19. An Ode to Bzip 10 upvotes, 1 comments
  20. Online astroturfing: A problem beyond disinformation 27 upvotes, 8 comments
  21. Montana passes Right to Compute act (2025) 141 upvotes, 93 comments
  22. RAM kits are now sold with one fake RAM stick alongside a real one 176 upvotes, 120 comments
  23. Recursive Problems Benefit from Recursive Solutions 40 upvotes, 23 comments
  24. Nominal Types in WebAssembly 23 upvotes, 12 comments
  25. Everything you never wanted to know about visually-hidden 9 upvotes, 3 comments
  26. Digg is gone again 326 upvotes, 333 comments
  27. XML Is a Cheap DSL 183 upvotes, 181 comments
  28. Starlink Militarization and Its Impact on Global Strategic Stability 63 upvotes, 74 comments
  29. Wired headphone sales are exploding 321 upvotes, 548 comments
